title: Pinalia acervata
taxonomy: Pinalia acervata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, specifically in the subtribe Aeridinae. The genus Pinalia belongs to the tribe Vandeae. The species Pinalia acervata is further classified into the section Vanda. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and taxonomic hierarchy of the plant. Pinalia acervata's classification provides valuable insights into its genetic and morphological characteristics.

intro: Pinalia acervata, also known as the Asian orchid, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia and China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia acervata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pinalia acervata is often prepared as a decoction or infused tea, as well as being formulated into capsules or tablets, to harness its potential health benefits.
